title: Kalaram Temple
tagline: Kalaram Temple — spiritual town of Maharashtra

overview.short: Kalaram Temple, is a Hindu temple in Nashik of Nashik district in Maharashtra, dedicated to the deity Rama.
overview.description: Kalaram Temple, is a Hindu temple in Nashik of Nashik district in Maharashtra, dedicated to the deity Rama. It is a temple situated in Panchavati area of the Nashik. The temple derives its name from a black statue of Rama. The literal translation of kalaram is "black Rama".

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Nashik
topPlaces.description: Nashik, formerly Nasik, is a city in the northern region of the Indian state of Maharashtra situated on the banks of the river Godavari, about 165 km (103 mi) northeast of the state capital Mumbai. The city lies in the Nashik Metropolitan Region.

topPlaces.name: Chatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Uddanpul
topPlaces.description: Chatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Uddanpul is in Nashik city, Dwarka area. It starts from Pandavleni caves and ends near Adgaon.

topPlaces.name: Nashik division
topPlaces.description: Nashik Division is one of the six divisions of India's Maharashtra state, and four of its five districts form Uttar Maharashtra. The three northernmost districts of Uttar Maharashtra form most of the historic Khandesh region, which covers the northern part of the division in the valley of the Tapti River.

topPlaces.name: Kapila River (Maharashtra)
topPlaces.description: The Kapila (Marathi: कपिला नदी) is a river in Maharashtra. It is a minor tributary of the Godavari River, India's second longest river after the Ganga.

topPlaces.name: Hutatma Anant Kanhere Maidan
topPlaces.description: Anant Kanhere Maidan is a cricket ground in Nashik, Maharashtra, India. Formerly the stadium was known as Golf Club Ground.

topPlaces.name: Guru Gobind Singh Polytechnic, Nasik
topPlaces.description: Guru Gobind Singh Foundation was set up in 1978 by the prominent and illustrious Punjabi residents of Nasik with the primary objective of providing quality education to the students irrespective of their caste, creed, religion or nationality so as to promote morality, patriotism, self-respect and self-confidence.

topPlaces.name: Gajpanth
topPlaces.description: Gajpanth (also spelled Gajpantha) is a Digambar Jain pilgrimage site (Siddha-kshetra) located in Mhasrul village, in the Nashik district of the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is managed and maintained by Registered Trust No.

topPlaces.name: Roman Catholic Diocese of Nashik
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Nashik (Latin: Nashiken(sis)) is a Catholic diocese located in the city of Nashik in the ecclesiastical province of Bombay in India.

items.note: Nashik, formerly Nasik, is a city in the northern region of the Indian state of Maharashtra situated on the banks of the river Godavari, about 165 km (103 mi) northeast of the state capital Mumbai. The city lies in the Nashik Metropolitan Region.
Chatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Uddanpul is in Nashik city, Dwarka area. It starts from Pandavleni caves and ends near Adgaon.

Nashik Division is one of the six divisions of India's Maharashtra state, and four of its five districts form Uttar Maharashtra. The three northernmost districts of Uttar Maharashtra form most of the historic Khandesh region, which covers the northern part of the division in the valley of the Tapti River.
The Kapila (Marathi: कपिला नदी) is a river in Maharashtra. It is a minor tributary of the Godavari River, India's second longest river after the Ganga.
Anant Kanhere Maidan is a cricket ground in Nashik, Maharashtra, India. Formerly the stadium was known as Golf Club Ground.

Guru Gobind Singh Foundation was set up in 1978 by the prominent and illustrious Punjabi residents of Nasik with the primary objective of providing quality education to the students irrespective of their caste, creed, religion or nationality so as to promote morality, patriotism, self-respect and self-confidence.

Gajpanth (also spelled Gajpantha) is a Digambar Jain pilgrimage site (Siddha-kshetra) located in Mhasrul village, in the Nashik district of the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is managed and maintained by Registered Trust No.
The Roman Catholic Diocese of Nashik (Latin: Nashiken(sis)) is a Catholic diocese located in the city of Nashik in the ecclesiastical province of Bombay in India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kalaram Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kalaram Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kalaram Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shirdi Airport (71 km) and the nearest railway station is Nashik Road (8 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kalaram Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kalaram Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kalaram Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kalaram Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kalaram Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Nashik, Chatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Uddanpul, Nashik division, Kapila River (Maharashtra), Hutatma Anant Kanhere Maidan.
